title: Friday Sermon 17 February 1989
description:
Sermons on Waqf e Nau: Some Guidelines for the Preparation of the new Generation of Devotees.

Every year at the time of International Bayat, thousands of people enter the religion of Allah in large groups.  Conversion to Ahmadiyya Islam is only the first step; it must be followed with the important task of training and mentoring. Hazrat Khalifatul Masih IV, in his extraordinary wisdom and foresight, envisioned this enormous task when he launched the Waqf e Nau scheme on April 3, 1987.

The Caliph requested parents to follow the traditions of the Prophets and offer their most prized possession - Their yet to be born children to the way of Allah. Devoted parents all over the world responded to the voice of their Imam and offered their children as devotees. This blessed scheme continues today as more parents offer their beautiful gifts to Allah.  The Waqf e Nau children are the devotees and leaders of the future who will train and lead the growing number of Ahmadi Muslims in the world. While these children will shoulder their responsibilities in the near future, we have an obligation today to prepare them for the enormous task that lies ahead.


The text http://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST19890217-UR.pdf
English Translation http://www.alislam.org/library/books/Sermons-on-Waqf-e-Nau.pdf#page=57

Nunspeet - Holland.
